orcas-island-tomorrow-forecast.content:
## 🏝️ Orcas Island Forecast – Friday, Feb 20

**Conditions:** Mixed snow/rain likely (90% chance), highs near 41°F dropping through the day.

**🚢 Ferry Travel:** FAVORABLE – Calm seas with 1 ft waves or less, light variable winds 5-8 kt. Good crossing conditions.

**🥾 Outdoor Activities:** Expect wet conditions all day. Morning snow possible before 10am may create slippery trails. Winds increase to 26 mph gusts by evening.

**⚠️ Heads Up:** Saturday brings stronger winds (31 mph gusts) and steady rain – plan outdoor activities for Friday if possible.
orcas-island-tomorrow-forecast.rawContent:

---

## 🌤️ Tomorrow's Forecast - Orcas Island, WA

### Temperature
| | Value |
|---------|-------|
| **High** | 41°F (falling to ~39°F in afternoon) |
| **Low** | 36°F (tonight: 30°F) |

### Precipitation
| | Details |
|---------|---------|
| **Chance** | **90%** |
| **Type** | Slight chance of snow before 10am, then **rain and snow mix** |
| **Accumulation** | Less than 0.1" rainfall expected |

### Wind Conditions ⚠️
| Period | Speed | Direction |
|--------|-------|-----------|
| **Daytime** | 6-10 mph | SE |
| **Evening** | 9-13 mph | ESE |
| **Gusts** | Up to **26 mph** by evening |

### Marine Conditions 🚢
| | Details |
|---------|---------|
| **Waves** | 1 ft or less |
| **Conditions** | Variable winds 5 kt or less, increasing to E 6-8 kt |
| **Advisory** | Calm seas - **favorable for ferry travel** |

---

### ⚠️ Travel Advisory
**Light winds and small waves** make for good ferry conditions, but expect **wet weather** throughout the day with potential for slippery conditions if snow mixes in during the morning hours. Conditions worsen Saturday (wind gusts to 31 mph, 100% rain).

seattle-tomorrow-forecast.content:
## Seattle Tomorrow (Fri, Feb 20)

**Morning:** Cold start near 30°F with frost. Partly cloudy skies, humidity around 70-74%. Bundle up!

**Afternoon:** Cloudy with temps reaching 37-39°F. Dry conditions continue with higher humidity (62-77%).

**Evening:** Partly cloudy, mild at 40-41°F. Comfortable humidity around 68-70%.

**Overall:** A cold, dry day with highs near 42°F and lows around 30°F. No rain expected. Light southerly winds 5-12 mph. Dress warmly for morning frost conditions.

### Summary:
Tomorrow will be a **cold, dry day** with temperatures hovering around freezing in the early morning and only reaching the low 40s by afternoon. Expect **clear skies overnight** transitioning to **mostly cloudy conditions** midday, then partly cloudy by evening. **No precipitation expected.** Light winds from the south (5-12 mph). Bundle up for frost in the morning!

# Washington Road Conditions Summary
**Feb 19-20, 2026**

**I-5 Seattle** ⚠️ DELAYS
2 NB lanes closed (Ship Canal Bridge). Express lanes NB only. Commutes 50+ min, 50% longer than normal.

**I-90 Snoqualmie Pass** ⚠️ CAUTION
Currently open. Heavy snow 80-100% chance Sat-Sun. Chain requirements likely.

**Highway 20 North Cascades** 🔴 CLOSED
Closed at MP 134. Reopens April/May.

**SR-20 to Anacortes** ✅ CLEAR
Ferry terminal accessible. Winter ferry schedule active.

**I-405** ⚠️ DELAYS
Weekend closures Feb 20-Mar 16. AM commutes 40-47 min.

**Highway 2 Stevens Pass** ✅ CLEAR
Fully reopened Feb 11 after flooding.

**Highway 99 First Ave S Bridge** ⚠️ CAUTION
2 NB lanes closed, 25 mph. Backup onto Hwy 509.

---
Check wsdot.com/travel for real-time updates.
wa-road-conditions.rawContent:
Now I have comprehensive information. Let me compile the road conditions report.

---

## 🚗 Washington State Road Conditions Report
**As of February 19-20, 2026**

---

### **I-5 (Seattle Corridor)** ⚠️ MAJOR DELAYS

**Active Construction - Revive I-5 Project:**
- **2 northbound lanes CLOSED** on the Ship Canal Bridge for the $178M repaving project
- Express lanes are running **northbound-only ALL DAY** during construction
- Project duration: Nearly all of 2026 and 2027

**Current Impact:**
- **Southbound morning commutes from Lynnwood to downtown Seattle:** Averaging **50+ minutes** (up from normal 35 min) - delays of ~50%
- **Southbound peak times (Tue-Fri):** Can exceed **65+ minutes**
- **Northbound is less impacted** due to express lanes pointing north
- **I-405 and Hwy 99** are also congested from diversion traffic

**Additional Alert (Feb 19):**
- **Highway 99/First Avenue South Bridge:** Right 2 northbound lanes CLOSED for maintenance, speed reduced to 25 mph. Traffic backing up onto Highway 509. No estimated reopening time.

---

### **I-90 (Snoqualmie Pass)** ⚠️ WINTER WEATHER ADVISORY

**Weather Forecast at Pass (elevation ~3,000 ft):**
- **Today:** 20% chance of snow, 24°F
- **Friday:** 30% chance of snow, highs near 27°F
- **Saturday-Sunday:** **Heavy snow likely (80-100% chance)**, accumulations of up to 1 inch possible each day
- **Next Week:** Prolonged snow pattern, possibly HEAVY snow Wed-Thu

**⛓️ Chain requirements may be implemented** – Carry chains and check conditions before travel.

---

### **Highway 20 (North Cascades Highway)** 🔴 CLOSED

**Status:** **PARTIALLY CLOSED** as of 2/18/2026
- Closed at **Milepost 134 (Ross Dam Trailhead)**
- Snow and avalanche hazards typically close the highway late November/early December
- Expected reopening: **April or early May** (weather dependent)

**Related Closures:**
- **Cascade River Road:** CLOSED (flooding/landslide damage from December storms)
- **Stehekin Valley Road:** CLOSED due to landslides – do not travel to Stehekin area

---

### **SR-20 to Anacortes (San Juan Islands Ferry Terminal)** ✅ OPEN

**Status:** The route from I-5 to Anacortes Ferry Terminal is **OPEN** and accessible.

**Ferry Information:**
- Washington State Ferries operating winter schedule
- **New (March 1, 2026):** 3% credit card fee on ticket purchases
- Current sailing season: **Winter schedule through March 21, 2026**
- Recommend checking [WSDOT Ferries](https://www.wsdot.wa.gov/ferries/schedule/) for real-time sailing status

---

### **I-405 Corridor** ⚠️ CONSTRUCTION + DELAYS

**Weekend Closures Coming (Feb 20-Mar 16):**
- **Feb 20 (Fri 9pm):** Westbound SR-522 lane closures for ramp demolition
- **Feb 27-Mar 2:** Both directions of SR-522 closed
- **Mar 6-9 & Mar 13-16:** Both directions of I-405 expected to close for demolition work

**Current Delays:**
- Southbound AM commutes averaging **40-47 minutes** (vs normal 30-33 min)
- Toll lanes charging up to **$15** during peak congestion

---

### **Highway 2 (Stevens Pass)** ✅ REOPENED

Good news: **Fully reopened Feb 11, 2026** after historic December flooding
- All sections from Skykomish to Leavenworth now open to normal two-way traffic
- Permanent repairs may require closures this summer

---

### 📱 Resources
- **WSDOT Real-Time Map:** wsdot.com/travel/real-time/map
- **Mountain Pass Conditions:** Check before crossing any pass
- **511 WA:** For traffic alerts

travel-summary.content:
**TRAVEL ADVISORY: Seattle → Orcas Island**
**Friday, February 20, 2026**

---

**Overall Recommendation: FAIR TRAVEL DAY** ⚠️

Travel is feasible with favorable ferry conditions, but significant delays and weather hazards require early departure and preparation.

---

**Key Concerns:**
- **I-5 Ship Canal Bridge:** 2 lanes closed, expect 50+ minute delays
- **Morning frost:** Seattle temps near 30°F, icy roads possible
- **Orcas weather:** 90% chance of mixed snow/rain on arrival

**Favorable Conditions:**
- SR-20 to Anacortes clear
- Calm seas with 1ft waves, light winds
- Ferry crossings operating normally

---

**Recommended Action:**
Depart Seattle by **6:00-6:30 AM** to avoid I-5 congestion and allow buffer for frost-related slowdowns. Confirm ferry reservation—winter schedule in effect.

---

**Pack List:**
- Warm layers, rain gear, waterproof footwear
- Gloves/hat for ferry deck
- Hot drinks/snacks for potential delays
- Ferry confirmation

---

**Planning Note:** Friday is the better travel day. Saturday forecast shows 31 mph gusts and steady rain—plan outdoor activities for Friday if possible.
travel-summary.rawContent:
# 🚗⛴️ Seattle → Orcas Island Travel Advisory
**Friday, February 20, 2026**

---

## 1️⃣ Current Conditions (as of Feb 19 evening)
- **Seattle:** 39°F, partly cloudy, light winds — comfortable conditions
- **Roads to Anacortes:** SR-20 is **CLEAR** ✅ — ferry terminal accessible

---

## 2️⃣ Tomorrow's Travel Outlook

| Segment | Conditions | Status |
|---------|------------|--------|
| **Seattle Morning** | 30°F with frost, partly cloudy | ⚠️ Icy roads possible |
| **I-5 Northbound** | 2 lanes closed at Ship Canal Bridge | ⚠️ 50+ min delays |
| **SR-20 to Anacortes** | Clear | ✅ Good |
| **Ferry Crossing** | Calm seas, 1ft waves, light winds | ✅ Favorable |
| **Orcas Island** | 90% chance mixed snow/rain, 41°F | ⚠️ Wet conditions |

---

## 3️⃣ Recommended Departure Time

**Leave Seattle by 6:00-6:30 AM** to:
- Beat I-5 congestion at Ship Canal Bridge
- Allow buffer for frost-related slowdowns
- Catch an early ferry (check winter schedule at wsdotfer.wa.gov)

---

## 4️⃣ What to Pack/Prepare

- 🧥 **Warm layers** — morning lows near 30°F
- 🌧️ **Rain gear** — 90% precipitation chance on Orcas
- 👢 **Waterproof footwear** — trails will be slippery
- 🧤 **Gloves/hat** — for ferry deck and outdoor activities
- ☕ **Hot drinks/snacks** — for potential delays
- 📱 **Ferry reservation confirmation** — winter schedule in effect

---

## 5️⃣ ⚠️ Alerts & Concerns

| Priority | Issue |
|----------|-------|
| 🔴 **HIGH** | Morning frost in Seattle — allow extra time, drive cautiously |
| 🔴 **HIGH** | I-5 Ship Canal Bridge lane closures — expect 50+ min delays |
| 🟡 **MEDIUM** | Morning snow possible on Orcas before 10am — slippery conditions |
| 🟡 **MEDIUM** | Evening wind gusts to 26 mph on Orcas — plan activities earlier |
| 🟢 **INFO** | Saturday forecast worse (31 mph gusts, steady rain) — Friday is better day |

---

**Bottom Line:** Travel is feasible tomorrow with good ferry conditions, but **leave early** to avoid I-5 delays and morning frost. Expect wet weather on arrival. If outdoor activities are planned, do them Friday — Saturday looks worse.
